# Scroll anchoring

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/search?q=Scroll%20anchoring%20CSS" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=Scroll%20anchoring" status="Scroll Anchoring 1" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Keep viewport position stable when content is inserted.

## Representative syntax

```css
overflow-anchor
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="Scroll anchoring" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="This demo uses a focusable scroll rail to show how scrolling features change the feel of carousels and reading panes." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use Scroll anchoring in floating UI when you need to keep a tooltip, popover, or menu attached to its trigger while the surrounding layout changes. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>For example, an overflow menu can stay aligned to a button and choose a safer placement near the viewport edge.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Pair it with a keyboard and focus plan. Placement can be CSS-driven, but opening, focus return, and dismissal still affect usability.</p>
</section>
</div>

## Source metadata

- Category: Scrolling, Overflow, and Scroll-based Components
- Maturity / source: Scroll Anchoring 1
- Representative syntax: `overflow-anchor`
- Position in category: 5 / 10
